tasks.name: comfyui-newbie-lumina-refactor

goals.goal: NewBie model is refactored to inherit from or integrate into Lumina's NextDiT class rather than being a standalone reimplementation. The code uses idiomatic ComfyUI operations (e.g. operations.Linear, operations.RMSNorm) and removes anti-pattern helper functions such as _pop_unexpected_kwargs and _fallback_operations. No kwargs popping or hardcoded fallback values.

The forward pass uses the Lumina convention for timesteps and return value: t = 1.0 - timesteps (not t = timesteps) and returns -img (not img).
No try/except block wrapping the forward logic (this breaks torch.compile). The forward path should use direct iteration or indexing instead.
No custom apply_model override on the NewBie model class. The standard BaseModel.apply_model pipeline should be used.
Conditioning for clip_text_pooled uses CONDRegular (not CONDCrossAttn). Pooled CLIP features are per-batch vectors, not cross-attention sequences.

Instead, a new dedicated method handles them with chaining semantics — handlers run in extension load order, each handler receives the event reflecting modifications from prior handlers (pass-by-reference / mutation pattern, not accumulator), handlers can return partial patches (content, details, isError) where omitted fields keep their current values, and the method returns undefined if no handler modified anything. The wrapper/call-site must invoke the new dedicated method instead of emit() for tool_result events.

Narrow the generic emit() method's event parameter type to exclude event types that have dedicated emitXxx() methods (e.g., tool_call, tool_result, user_bash, context, before_agent_start, resources_discover, input). The emit() signature should only accept events that do NOT have their own specialized emission method, providing stronger compile-time type safety.

Clean up the session_before special-case handling in emit(): improve the type guard and result types for session_before events so the special-case block is cleaner and more type-safe (e.g., the type guard should operate on the event object rather than a raw string, result types should cover all session_before variants, return type should be properly narrowed).

Update the extensions documentation to describe the tool_result chaining behavior: handlers run in extension load order, each handler sees the latest result after previous handler changes, handlers can return partial patches where omitted fields keep their current values.
Fix test.sh to exclude additional API keys (specifically KIMI_API_KEY and HF_TOKEN) so tests don't fail due to environment variable leakage.
